reference_answers.full_answer: The LHCb DaVinci project has ID 544, namespace path 'lhcb/DaVinci', and default branch 'master'. URL: https://gitlab.cern.ch/lhcb/DaVinci

facts.description: The LHCb project contains general purpose classes used throughout the LHCb software. It is built on top of the Gaudi framework.

reference_answers.full_answer: The project is lhcb/LHCb with ID 399. It contains general purpose classes used throughout LHCb software and is built on top of the Gaudi framework.
DaVinci uses CMake as its build system with a minimum required version of 3.15, as specified in CMakeLists.txt.
The subdirectories registered in DaVinci's CMakeLists.txt are: DaVinciExamples, DaVinciTutorials, DaVinciSys, DaVinciTests, Phys/DaVinci, Phys/FunTuple, HltEfficiencyChecker, and DaVinciCache.
DaVinci's CI/CD pipeline has stages: test, build, docs, and deploy. Jobs include python-linting, build, build-docs, and pages, running on alma9-devel container with CVMFS tags.

facts.copyright_holder: CERN for the benefit of the LHCb Collaboration

reference_answers.full_answer: DaVinci uses GNU GPL version 3 license. This is stated in the CMakeLists.txt and .gitlab-ci.yml file headers, and verbatim in the COPYING file. Copyright belongs to CERN for the benefit of the LHCb Collaboration.

facts.purpose: Modular dependency management using CMake's include mechanism

reference_answers.full_answer: The cmake/ directory contains DaVinciDependencies.cmake, which suggests modular dependency management. The main CMakeLists.txt includes it via include(DaVinciDependencies).
According to project inspection, DaVinci supports C++ and Fortran ecosystems, uses CMake as build system, with C++ as the primary language.
Connection test shows: GitLab URL is https://gitlab.cern.ch, authentication is false (no token provided), public access is available, status is connected.

facts.purpose: An open project for providing interfaces and services for building HEP experiment frameworks
facts.scope: Experiment independent - used for event data processing applications across multiple experiments

reference_answers.full_answer: The main Gaudi project is gaudi/Gaudi with ID 38. It provides interfaces and services for building HEP experiment frameworks. It is experiment-independent, used for event data processing across multiple experiments. URL: https://gitlab.cern.ch/gaudi/Gaudi
